heading: Key Takeaway
text: Work done on an object is defined as the magnitude of the force multiplied by the distance moved by the object in the direction of the applied force. The unit of work is joule: 1 joule = 1 newton 1 metre.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Work done on an object by a force would be zero if the displacement of the object is zero.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: An object having capability to do work is said to possess energy. Energy has the same unit as that of work.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: An object in motion possesses what is known as the kinetic energy of the object. An object of mass, m moving with velocity v has a kinetic energy of mv2 .

heading: Key Takeaway
text: The energy possessed by a body due to its change in position or shape is called the potential energy. The gravitational potential energy of an object of mass, m raised through a height, h from the earth s surface is given by m g h.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: According to the law of conservation of energy, energy can only be transformed from one form to another; it can neither be created nor destroyed. The total energy before and after the transformation always remains constant.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Energy exists in nature in several forms such as kinetic energy, potential energy, heat energy, chemical energy etc.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: The sum of the kinetic and potential energies of an object is called its mechanical energy.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Power is defined as the rate of doing work. The SI unit of power is watt. 1 W = 1 J/s.
